title: Install progress bar overshoots — pip requirements bytes counted in numerator but not denominator (203MB / 15MB)
description: DISPLAY bug (not a failure — the install completes fine), seen live + recurring across sessions. During a custom-node dep install the 'Installing Dependencies' modal shows an impossible ratio: e.g. '0.3 MB / 15.0 MB' during the git-archive download, then jumps to '203.0 MB / 15.0 MB' once the phase flips to 'Installing custom node requirements...'. The numerator overshoots the denominator by >10x. ROOT (to confirm at fix time): the model job's totalBytes (denominator) is seeded from the git-archive DOWNLOAD size only (dep.size / seedBytes — the ~15MB node zip), but when the phase moves into installRequirements the pip install pulls ~200MB of wheels and those bytes get accumulated into downloadedBytes (the numerator) via the download:progress broadcast, WITHOUT ever being added to totalBytes. So numerator = archive-download + pip-download; denominator = archive-download only → the bar reads >100%. Site: routes/downloadManager.js — the ResumableDownloader / _runCustomNodeInstall progress path (download:progress broadcast around :288-326 sets downloadedBytes/totalBytes from stats; the custom-node requirements phase around :304-314 reuses the same {indeterminate, phase} contract). Surfaced during the MPI-222 RES4LYF baked-node drift test (2026-07-08, RES4LYF pip reqs = numpy/opencv/matplotlib/scipy/pillow ~203MB). FIX OPTIONS (pick at fix time): (a) during the pip/requirements phase switch the bar to INDETERMINATE (the {indeterminate:true} contract already exists — pip has no honest total ahead of time), OR (b) stop feeding pip bytes into downloadedBytes and keep the numerator to the download total, OR (c) if a real pip total can be resolved, add it to totalBytes too so both sides agree. (a) is the lazy/correct default — pip byte totals are not known up-front, so a determinate bar is a lie regardless. Local + remote both use this progress path; check both. Low priority — cosmetic, install works.
